programme.title: Space's Deepest Secrets: Mystery of the Alien Asteroid
programme.desc: When a strange alien object is discovered speeding through the solar system, experts race to uncover if it's real evidence of extraterrestrial contact, or a deadly asteroid on a collision course with planet earth.

programme.title: Space's Deepest Secrets: Secrets of the Seven Earths
programme.desc: The new discovery of seven alien Earth-like planets in a faraway solar system is a major milestone in our hunt for extraterrestrial life, and experts investigate the secrets of TRAPPIST-1's mysterious worlds to reveal if we're truly alone in the universe.

programme.title: NASA's Unexplained Files: Secrets of the Alien Signals
programme.desc: NASA begins a strange experiment to communicate with dolphins, and experts investigate if it's a prelude to making alien contact. However, some fear that such a discovery could have apocalyptic consequences.

programme.title: NASA's Unexplained Files: Aliens in the Amazon
programme.desc: After Neil Armstrong's moonwalk, he led an expedition into the Amazon to find a legendary library that holds all knowledge of an advanced alien civilization. Experts ask if a close encounter on the moon's surface inspired Armstrong's mysterious quest.

programme.title: NASA's Unexplained Files
programme.desc: Take a look at some of the strange and unexplained cases NASA has encountered.

programme.title: NASA's Unexplained Files: Planet of the Metal Aliens
programme.desc: After a strange discovery confirms that life is possible on its surface, scientists begin to investigate if a nearby exoplanet could be home to an alien civilization.

programme.title: NASA's Unexplained Files: Interstellar Gold Rush
programme.desc: A distant planet shows evidence of an Earth-like atmosphere, while a mysterious glowing light suggests the impossible - an atmosphere on the Moon. Have we found evidence of a crashed alien ship on the asteroid belt and why are there blueberries on Mars?

programme.title: NASA's Unexplained Files: Aliens vs. Stalin at Area 51
programme.desc: When a former Area 51 insider alleges that the Roswell UFO incident was a nefarious Soviet conspiracy masterminded by Stalin, experts investigate if it was indeed an enemy plot against America, or whether the confession is just another cover-up.

programme.title: NASA's Unexplained Files: Planet of the Metal Aliens
programme.desc: After a strange discovery confirms that life is possible on its surface, scientists begin to investigate if a nearby exoplanet could be home to an alien civilization.

programme.title: NASA's Unexplained Files: Did We Nuke Jupiter?
programme.desc: Find out if NASA could be responsible for a nuclear explosion on Jupiter. Also, new evidence could reveal the truth behind the mystery of the iconic face on Mars. Plus, learn about an unknown force that crashed an Apollo spacecraft on the moon.

programme.title: Unearthed: Lost City of Troy
programme.desc: The legendary lost city at the center of the Trojan War is Troy, but its existence has never been confirmed. Now, discoveries and cutting-edge science might prove if this place existed, and if the Greeks really conquered it with the infamous Trojan Horse.

programme.title: Unearthed: Mount Rushmore: The Hidden Secrets
programme.desc: Delving into the dark secrets behind one of the most iconic landmarks in America: Mount Rushmore. Scientists reveal the secret history of its creation, and what lies behind the rock.

programme.title: Unearthed: Egypt's Buried City
programme.desc: There's a lost city buried in the Sahara, and evidence reveals it's home to Nefertiti, Tutankhamun, and the forbidden god of an outlawed religion. Experts uncover the secret doomsday that caused this strange place to disappear.

programme.title: Unearthed: Tower of Babel: The New Evidence
programme.desc: The Tower of Babel is one of the most famous stories of the Bible, and to find out if it really existed, archaeologists use cutting-edge tech and the latest science to investigate how ancient engineers could have built such an extraordinary megastructure.

programme.title: Unearthed: Vesuvius's Secret Victim
programme.desc: Scientists are using cutting edge technology to unearth a second city that, like the well-known city of Pompeii, was destroyed when Vesuvius erupted in 79 AD. This new town is revealed to be one of the most well-preserved Roman towns on earth.

programme.title: Unearthed: Hitler's Lost Bunker
programme.desc: Adolf Hitler's underground bunker was the home of some of the greatest secrets of the Nazi's. Now, experts use cutting-edge tech to discover new evidence of Hitler's evil vision for global domination.

programme.title: Egypt's Unexplained Files: Mystery of the Cannibal Crypt
programme.desc: Mysterious tomb carvings provide dark evidence that ancient Egyptians practiced cannibalism, but we don't know why. New discoveries might finally answer this ancient mystery, and experts use modern-day tech to reveal more of Egypt's darkest secrets.

programme.title: Egypt's Unexplained Files: Curse of the Crocodile Queen
programme.desc: The Crocodile Queen was Egypt's first female pharaoh, but we don't who she was, or why she took this sinister alter-ego. New discoveries might finally answer this ancient mystery, and more of Egypt's darkest secrets.

programme.title: Egypt's Unexplained Files: Secrets of the Tomb Raiders
programme.desc: New discoveries reveal the secrets of Egypt's tomb raiders, and what ancient Egyptians did to ensure the treasures of their dead pharaohs wouldn't be stolen. The latest science answers these great mysteries, shedding new light on Egypt's darkest secrets.

programme.title: Egypt's Unexplained Files: Secrets of the Scorpion King
programme.desc: New evidence suggests that the mythological Scorpion King might have actually existed. New discoveries might answer this ancient mystery, and experts use modern-day tech to reveal more of Egypt's darkest secrets.

programme.title: Egypt's Unexplained Files: Tut's Curse: The New Evidence
programme.desc: New discoveries reveal if King Tut's tomb is indeed cursed, as it is said to have killed nine people. The latest science might finally answer this ancient mystery, and experts use modern-day tech to investigate more of Egypt's darkest secrets.

programme.title: Egypt's Unexplained Files: Lost City of the Sun Cult
programme.desc: When experts unearth a mysterious lost city built by an infamous pharaoh, they find clues of a grisly catastrophe. New discoveries reveal the apocalyptic events that turned this place into hell on earth, offering more clues to Egypt's darkest secrets.

programme.title: Dallas SWAT
programme.desc: Steve Claggett and Rich Emberlin are called out to help execute an arrest warrant on a gang of suspected crack-cocaine dealers barricaded inside a house; the SWAT team's tactical skills are put to the test as they overcome the barriers-to-entry.

programme.title: Dallas SWAT
programme.desc: Memorial Day weekend erupts when SWAT is called in to subdue a home intruder, whose startling method of entry leaves the team scratching their heads. An alleged heroin dealer is scoped out by an officer who's on a mission to put him out of business.

programme.title: The Big House: Colorado State

programme.title: The Big House: Maze Prison
programme.desc: The Maze Prison held Catholic and Protestant prisoners, along with paramilitary groups.

programme.title: The Big House: Attica Prison
programme.desc: This prison was hailed as a model for the confinement of the most dangerous criminals.

programme.title: The Big House: Auburn
programme.desc: The State Penitentiary in Auburn, New York is a monument to criminal rehabilitation.

programme.title: The Big House: Montana State Prison
programme.desc: Today, the state prison in Deer Lodge, Montana operates as both a ranch and a modern high-security prison but its legacy is dark and its history is marked by violent uprisings.

programme.title: Nightwatch Nation: Not Today
programme.desc: Yonkers first responders face unexpected challenges when they respond to a call for a man with a broken ankle who fell from a fire escape. In Tucson, Steven and Sam are called to a structure fire and must search inside for possible victims.

programme.title: Fugitive Chronicles: Danny Ray Horning
programme.desc: Horning goes to the one place he thinks he'll never be found, the Grand Canyon. Living off the land, he evades capture in the longest and largest manhunt in Arizona state history.

programme.title: Fugitive Chronicles: Darli
programme.desc: An incredible international manhunt ensues when a player in Miami's drug world goes on the lam.

programme.title: Female Forces: For Better or for Worse
programme.desc: Officer Dawn Yorke tries to settle domestic dispute with a wife who refuses to let her abusive husband back into their home. Next, Sgt. Betsy Brantner-Smith gives a good lecture to a husband who gets pulled over after bickering with his wife.

programme.title: Female Forces: Ready to Rumble
programme.desc: The women of Naperville PD show just how tough they are as they combat out of control suspects including a kid who tries to charge one of the officers and a mother with a violent daughter seeking advice.

programme.title: The Lake Erie Murders: Who Killed Amy Mihaljevic?: Part 2: The Hunt
programme.desc: Amy's killer remains at large. A cavalcade of suspects emerges, each more compelling than the last. Investigators explore each one. Then, 106 days after Amy was taken, a gruesome discovery some 50 miles away from her Bay Village home: a body.

programme.title: The Lake Erie Murders: Who Killed Amy Mihaljevic?: Part 3: New Evidence
programme.desc: Amy's killer remains on the loose and the case goes nationwide. Margaret appears on Oprah, anniversaries come and go, and then, finally, a confession. A man stands up in church in a neighboring town and declares: 'I killed Amy Mihaljevic.'

programme.title: The Lake Erie Murders: The Butcher of Buffalo
programme.desc: Mother of three Joan Giambra is discovered strangled, lying naked under her comatose 11-year old daughter. The young girl becomes the only hope of catching a serial killer.

programme.title: The Lake Erie Murders: High School Horror
programme.desc: When the body of beautiful high school student Debbie Gama is discovered in a creek wrapped in wire, the ensuing investigation ends up uncovering more than her murderer, revealing a secret world of twisted sex, drugs, sadomasochism and bondage.

programme.title: The Lake Erie Murders: Alley of Death
programme.desc: When 14-year old Gloria Pointer is found raped and murdered in an alleyway, it is the start of a thirty-year quest for answers by her mother who will not give up until she finds justice for her daughter.

programme.title: The Night That Didn't End: Missing Half
programme.desc: In Dayton, Ohio, a young mother goes missing after attending a party late one evening. As her whereabouts remain unknown for days, the missing woman's identical twin is convinced there is something deeply sinister at play.

programme.title: People Magazine Investigates: Nine Years in the Shadows
programme.desc: When a young woman is found murdered in her Modesto, CA home in 1988, police investigate the strife and infidelity on both sides of her marriage. But it's a surprising witness who eventually leads police to her killer.

programme.title: People Magazine Investigates: A Killing in Chinatown
programme.desc: Art imitates life when Cambodian genocide survivor Dr. Haing Ngor wins an Oscar for his role in the film The Killing Fields. But when he's gunned down the LAPD investigate a web of political intrigue, revenge and street gangs to uncover the truth.

programme.title: The Night That Didn't End: Missing Half
programme.desc: In Dayton, Ohio, a young mother goes missing after attending a party late one evening. As her whereabouts remain unknown for days, the missing woman's identical twin is convinced there is something deeply sinister at play.

programme.title: People Magazine Investigates: Nine Years in the Shadows
programme.desc: When a young woman is found murdered in her Modesto, CA home in 1988, police investigate the strife and infidelity on both sides of her marriage. But it's a surprising witness who eventually leads police to her killer.

programme.title: People Magazine Investigates: A Killing in Chinatown
programme.desc: Art imitates life when Cambodian genocide survivor Dr. Haing Ngor wins an Oscar for his role in the film The Killing Fields. But when he's gunned down the LAPD investigate a web of political intrigue, revenge and street gangs to uncover the truth.

programme.title: The Lake Erie Murders: Alley of Death
programme.desc: When 14-year old Gloria Pointer is found raped and murdered in an alleyway, it is the start of a thirty-year quest for answers by her mother who will not give up until she finds justice for her daughter.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter: Lt. Joe Kenda: Stuck in a Coffin
programme.desc: In the aftermath of a five-alarm fire, first responders discover a lifeless body. When Kenda learns that the deadly blaze was no accident, he launches a manhunt for a pyromaniac with a fiery temper and a callous disregard for human life.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter: Lt. Joe Kenda: Too Young to Die
programme.desc: An ambush at a hamburger stand leaves a 16-year old dead. Lt. Joe Kenda must cut through rumor and innuendo in order to zero in on his prime suspect. But before he can catch the killer, a surprise witness blows the case wide open.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter: Lt. Joe Kenda: Rearview Mirror
programme.desc: When a retired military man runs over a pedestrian, it's up to Lt. Joe Kenda to determine if it was bad brakes or bad intent. Then A shooting in a dive bar leads to a quick arrest, but Kenda can't quite shake the feeling that something is amiss.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter: Lt. Joe Kenda: Settle the Score
programme.desc: When Johnny Crawford is killed in his easy chair, police are perplexed to learn he may have been shot over a musical instrument. But as Lt. Joe Kenda digs deeper, it soon becomes clear the young man wasn't killed for his own sins...but someone else's.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter: Lt. Joe Kenda: My Case to Solve
programme.desc: A jealous vendetta leaves a popular DJ dead, and his killer has dropped off the map. As a newly promoted supervisor, Sgt. Joe Kenda gambles his career on a risky strategy to bring his prime suspect to justice.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter: Lt. Joe Kenda: End of Days
programme.desc: The discovery of a family home littered with corpses tasks Kenda with unspooling the paranoid obsessions of a diseased mind, and forces him to confront his greatest fear. Then a deadly beating a cheap motel illuminates the dark underbelly of love.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter: Lt. Joe Kenda: A Prayer for the Dying
programme.desc: When a squabble at a store turns violent, Raymond Archuleta steps in to help- only to be stabbed through the heart. Lt Joe Kenda must solve the case with little evidence besides a bloody bouquet, and a sketch of the mystery woman last seen holding them.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter: Lt. Joe Kenda: Fractured Glass
programme.desc: When affable ladies' man Michael Tittemore is gunned down in the dead of night, Kenda first sets his sights on the roommates who were present during the murder. But a shocking revelation will turn his attention to a far more nefarious band of suspects.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter: Lt. Joe Kenda: Shattered
programme.desc: When two teens discover a lifeless body in the street, Lt. Joe Kenda must determine if the killer was a jealous lover or just plain unhinged. Then Kenda turns to a forensic expert to see if a young mother could possibly have hung herself.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter: Lt. Joe Kenda: Drive Thru Murder
programme.desc: Hard-working fast food manager Missy Berry was found dead in an idling car at a deserted intersection.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter: Lt. Joe Kenda: Sacrificial Lamb
programme.desc: When thirteen-year-old PJ Grant is found dead from a single gunshot wound to the head, Lt. Joe Kenda assumes it's a suicide. But when the physical evidence doesn't add up, it opens the door to a world of possibilities-from hatred to human sacrifice.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter: Lt. Joe Kenda: The Line Goes Dead
programme.desc: When the severed head of a wife and mother is found, Lt. Joe Kenda uses forensics and interrogation to find both her body and her killer. Then... a young man's murder looks like a robbery gone wrong until Kenda learns the odd reason he's living there.

programme.title: Ultimate Weapons: Firepower
programme.desc: Take a look at some of the world's most powerful man-portable weapons. Psychological impact and the power to intimidate, firepower systems have the military capability to unleash tremendous force on the enemy.

programme.title: Ultimate Weapons: Robotic Warriors
programme.desc: Robotic Warriors are futuristic unmanned war machines that can even think for themselves. These intelligent weapon systems combine stealth and autonomy with the power to strike and destroy their targets.

programme.title: Ultimate Weapons: Shock and Awe
programme.desc: Shock and Awe weapons are designed to make a massive impact. These rapid dominance systems overpower in the most effective ways, physically and psychologically overtaking the enemy.

programme.title: Ultimate Weapons: Close Quarter Battle
programme.desc: Explore the chosen weapons when engaging the enemy at short range. The decision could mean the difference between life and death.

programme.title: What History Forgot: To the Ends of the World
programme.desc: Delve into the unknown stories behind some of the biggest disasters, threats and conquests in America's skies. From the attack on Pearl Harbor, the Hindenburg disaster, Apollo 13 and the race to the Moon.

programme.title: What History Forgot: Verge of Destruction
programme.desc: We've all heard about the fallout shelters used for refuge during World War II, the deadly firebombing of Tokyo, and the D-Day invasion on the beaches of Normandy but there are more secrets.

programme.title: What History Forgot: Secret Weapons
programme.desc: Discover the unknown stories of some of the incredible plots, ploys and people behind America's secret weapons during the Cold War, the Vietnam War, and WWII.

programme.title: What History Forgot: Rebels With a Cause
programme.desc: Rosa parks was not the first African American women to refuse to give up her seat. The Berlin Airlift included candy for kids. One of the great rifles used in war was invented by a man who made moonshine.

programme.desc: We've all heard about the fallout shelters used for refuge during World War II, the deadly firebombing of Tokyo, and the D-Day invasion on the beaches of Normandy but there are more secrets.

programme.title: Nazi Fugitives: Adolf Eichmann
programme.desc: This is the hunt for Adolf Eichmann, considered by many to be one of the main architects of the Holocaust.

programme.title: Nazi Fugitives: Erich Priebke
programme.desc: A half-century after the war, American journalists track down former Gestapo officer Erich Priebke. Priebke, who was responsible for some of Italy's worst atrocities, fled to Argentina after the war.

programme.title: Nazi Fugitives: Paul Touvier
programme.desc: French investigators hunt down Paul Touvier, a pro-Nazi sympathizer who was responsible for murdering both Jews and resistance fighters. This 'Hangman of Lyon' hid himself with the assistance of a right wing group associated with the Catholic Church.

programme.title: Big Texas Fix: Fire Salvaged Victorian
programme.desc: Ashley and Michael see hidden potential in a fire-damaged Victorian and decide to save it from demolition. They open up the cramped space by turning a breezeway into a kitchen and repurpose an old safe.

programme.title: Big Texas Fix: Dream House
programme.desc: Michael and Ashley stumble upon a pre-1900s Victorian after some curbside dumpster diving. They hit New Orleans for some perfect vintage decor then return to combine two rooms into one spacious kitchen and make this the dream house worth of the name.

programme.title: Big Texas Fix: The Kettle House
programme.desc: Michael and Ashley get the opportunity to renovate a bizarre Galveston icon that may harbor a few surprises inside. The spaceship-shaped landmark is soon brimming with life as the Cordrays bring their unique charm to the one-of-a-kind house.

programme.title: Big Texas Fix: Mystery House
programme.desc: Michael surprises Ashley with an 1893 raised cottage that he purchased before seeing the full interior. The duo soon finds themselves in over their heads when they realize it's not quite the hidden gem Michael thought it was.

programme.title: Salvage Dawgs: Salvaging Items From an Apple Orchard into Repurposed Goods
programme.desc: The Black Dog Salvage crew head into the mountains to salvage different structures of an old apple orchard. Key finds include roof tin, apple picking ladders, and apple crates. Robert also helps Ted build a commissioned iron gate.

programme.title: Salvage Dawgs: Salvaging Items in Roanoke, VA, Into Repurposed Goods
programme.desc: At the warehouse, Mike builds a coffee table from a panel door.

programme.title: Salvage Dawgs: Virginia Tobacco Plant
programme.desc: Robert takes the team to tobacco country where they tackle a cigarette factory, without an elevator, on the hottest day of the year. With the Dawg Bowl opening just around the corner, Mike and Tay shift their focus to a lovable piece for the venue.

programme.title: Salvage Dawgs: Salvaging Items in Boardman, Oh, into Repurposed Goods
programme.desc: Mike builds a bed frame from horse jump poles; the crew brings in an expert.

programme.title: Salvage Dawgs: Salvaging Items From Columbus, Ohio Into Repurposed Goods
programme.desc: The guys tackle disassembling a 40 foot Eiffel Tower replica in Columbus. While there, they meet a fellow salvager and pick through his collection. Grayson also builds a floor lamp with salvaged materials back at the warehouse.

programme.title: Salvage Dawgs: St. Mary's Rectory
programme.desc: The team finds cast-iron mantles, a built-in cabinet, and a stained glass front doorway.

programme.title: Salvage Dawgs: American Viscose Plant
programme.desc: The Dawgs save a kiln from the American Viscose Plant in Roanoke, VA. Mike and Tay work together to build custom tables for a local optometrist office. Will Farmer also returns to appraise a map from 1853.

programme.title: Salvage Dawgs: Beechcraft Airplane Salvage
programme.desc: The Salvage Dawgs try something a little different by bidding on a 1947 Beechcraft airplane. Mike goes a little over budget to win the auction and the crew has to hire professionals to figure out how to disassemble it properly.

programme.title: Paid Program
programme.desc: Paid programming.

programme.title: Alaska Flip N Move: Cozy Lake Cabin Almost Becomes an Icy Underwater House
programme.desc: The Sikes family takes a chance on a lake house that has potential but may be too hard to move. They run into a major snag when they try and take the shortest route for the move, which happens to be straight across a frozen lake.

programme.title: Alaska Flip N Move: Barn Top vs. Container Cabin
programme.desc: Flipping friends Bob and Jubal take the empty top half of an old barn and turn it into a modern, open-concept living space. Meanwhile, the Merricks get a bargain on a shipping container, but they have to move it down an icy mountain road.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters: Taking It to the Finish Line
programme.desc: Chase and the team are hired to finish a cabin for a homeowner that's been collecting materials for years but just hasn't been able to finish the project.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters: A Dream Come True
programme.desc: A couple with five children calls on the Cabin Masters to take their newly purchased camp on David Pond and turn it into a family vacation cabin that will serve as a central part of their lives for generations to come.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters: Ho-Ho-Home!
programme.desc: Doug, aka Santa, and his wife Lois inherited a 1,250-square-foot cabin that's been in her family since 1958. With no major renovations since the 1970s, Chase and the team are excited to perform a Christmas miracle and transform this cabin.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters: City Slickers Off the Grid
programme.desc: Chase and his team of builders renovate an off-the-grid camp for a family of city slickers. With limited resources, Chase and his team work to incorporate the comforts of city living into the camp.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters: Heirloom Home on the Shore
programme.desc: After 40 years of use, third-generation owners have decided to add facilities and living quarters to a family camp for large annual family reunions.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters: Inspirations Brewing
programme.desc: When Chase and his brother-in-law learn that the owner of their latest cabin project works at Allagash Brewing Company, they're excited to check out the brewery for inspiration as they turn the weekend getaway into a three-season home away from home.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters: A Cabin for the Bride
programme.desc: Chase and his team are back home in Augusta getting ready for Lance's wedding. Chase gets a call about a little log cabin stuck in a backyard. He sees the cabin as a fun project for the team to work on and present to Lance's lovely bride.

programme.title: Carnival Eats: Beauty and the Beef
programme.desc: It's love at first bite as Noah Cappe munches on some Texas-sized treats at Rodeo Austin. With a tagline of "where Western meets weird," the Rodeo is the place to find one-of-a-kind carnival eats like the Kobe beef Rodeo Austin burger.

programme.title: Carnival Eats: Mocktail
programme.desc: Noah Cappe goes loco for Sunshine State snacks at the Manatee County Fair in Palmetto, Fla. There's lots of ooey-gooey goodness in the pizza supremo burger and the ulti-melt burger, but it's the sandia loca that has Noah flipping out.

programme.title: Carnival Eats: There Will Be Bacon
programme.desc: Noah Cappe pigs out at the Bacon and Bourbon Fest in Wellington, Fla., where all-American classics make an appearance in the stuffed crust bacon-za, but it's the peanut butter pork donuts that hit the sweet and salty spot.

programme.title: Carnival Eats: Planet Of The Dates
programme.desc: Noah Cappe is ready for 1,001 nights of fun at the Arabian Nights-themed Riverside County Fair and National Date Festival in Indio, Calif. He makes a date with a dangerous burger and enjoys a sweet mashup with the churro dog.

programme.title: Man v. Food: Worcester, MA
programme.desc: Casey Webb lands in Worcester, MA, where he slurps down some radical ramen, indulges in peanut butter cup French toast and takes on a rack of deep-fried ribs with the heat of a million Scoville units.

programme.title: Man v. Food: San Diego, CA
programme.desc: Casey travels to San Diego to face off against a five and a half pound stack of pancakes layered with bacon, eggs, sausage, country-fried steak and thick-cut ham.

programme.title: Man v. Food: Milwaukee, WI
programme.desc: Casey travels to Milwaukee for some epic Brew City eats, including a pitcher-sized Bloody Mary garnished with a whole chicken and a four-and-a-half-pound Reuben sandwich challenge.

programme.title: Man v. Food: Sacramento, California
programme.desc: Casey Webb swings into Sacramento, California, for three gold-standard meals from the Golden State, including a perfectly fried Chinese-American chicken, a savory multi-meat waffle stack.

programme.title: The Best Thing I Ever Ate: Holiday Delights
programme.desc: Nothing says 'Happy Holidays' like having all your favourite festive foods in one place. Brad Miller finds the Yuletide spirit with his favorite holiday steak from Harry's Savoy Grill in Wilmington, Del.

programme.title: The Best Thing I Ever Ate: Mexican Favorites
programme.desc: We're saluting magnificent Mexican food from all around the country starting with Alton Brown's favorite homemade salsa in Columbus, Ohio. Ali Khan then serves up a true Mexican original with beef carnitas tacos in Austin, Texas.

programme.title: The Best Thing I Ever Ate: Pie's the Limit
programme.desc: From savory to sweet and cheesy to meaty, the pie possibilities are endless. Wolfgang Puck picks a perfect fruit and cream pie in Los Angeles.

programme.title: The Best Thing I Ever Ate: Terrific Italian
programme.desc: Celebrity chefs and Cooking Channel personalities are talking about the most sensational Italian food they've ever had. Alton Brown revisits a decadent, old-world pasta dish.

programme.title: The Best Thing I Ever Ate: Comforting Classics
programme.desc: There's nothing quite as comforting as a classic, down-home meal that reminds you of the good old days. For Ted Allen, it's a cinnamon roll, and for Giada De Laurentiis, it has to be pot pie.

programme.title: The Best Thing I Ever Ate: Southern Eats
programme.desc: It's time for some good, old-fashioned country cooking as Guy Fieri, Alton Brown, Sunny Anderson and Duff Goldman round up their favorite Southern eats.

programme.title: Food's Greatest Hits: BBQ Bonanza
programme.desc: Grab some napkins and put on that bib for this finger lickin' episode featuring ribs, brisket and traditional sides (collard greens, potato salad and baked beans).

programme.title: Food's Greatest Hits: Super Sandwiches
programme.desc: Three hundred million of these are eaten every day in America and we celebrates the greatest thing since sliced bread, the sandwich. We tip our cap to the Earl of Sandwich as we look at the po boy, the Philly cheesesteak and the ice cream sandwich.

programme.title: Food's Greatest Hits: Snack Attack
programme.desc: Come spoil your dinner with us as we attack some of food's greatest snacks deep-fried bites, chips and dip, plus everyone's fave, popcorn.

programme.title: Food's Greatest Hits: Sugar Rush
programme.desc: We're looking at three of food's sweetest hits including ice cream sundaes and cupcakes.

programme.title: Food's Greatest Hits: Super Cheesy
programme.desc: Outrageously cheesy dishes in all their melty glory get the all-star treatment, with macaroni and cheese, grilled cheese sandwiches and cheesecake front and center.

programme.title: Food's Greatest Hits: Diner Delights
programme.desc: Food's Greatest Hits gets its diner on, serving up three quintessentially American dishes: juicy burgers, sky-high club sandwiches, and creamy milkshakes.

programme.title: Cheap Eats: Charleston
programme.desc: Ali heads south to Charleston and starts the day off right with creamy grits topped with all the morning classics. Then a crab cake sandwich that's the talk of the town, followed by seafood-stuffed hushpuppies.

programme.title: Cheap Eats: Miami
programme.desc: Ali travels to Miami and breaks his fast with a classic Cuba staple: empanadas. He then samples some fruits of the sea, with right-off-the-boat mahi mahi sandwiches and a fisherman's stew.

programme.title: Cheap Eats: Albuquerque
programme.desc: While Albuquerque is known for hatch green chile and New Mex cuisine, Ali Khan discovers a lot more behind the hype. Ali goes sweet on a vegan waffle that delivers like a decadent desert; then makes way for the best green chile cheeseburger in town.

programme.title: Cheap Eats: Chicago
programme.desc: Ali Khan's Windy City expectations are as high as Chicago's Willis Tower, so his breakfast is a tower of fried chicken, biscuits, egg and bacon. For lunch, Ali dives into the city's unofficial sandwich, the Italian beef.

programme.title: Cheap Eats: Great Lakes Craves
programme.desc: Ali Kahn digs into the best deals in Grand Rapids with a twilight French toast glistening with creamy anglaise sauce. For lunch, it's a Mexican fiesta with layers of juicy pork torta de jamon.

programme.title: Cheap Eats: Mountain Feast
programme.desc: Ali hits the Jackson Hole, Wyo., trail running for a delicious croque madame with mornay sauce in the morning and crosses the Snake River for a crunchy fried chicken banh mi sandwich.

programme.title: Cheap Eats: Fillin' Up in the South
programme.desc: Ali Khan hits the ground running in Richmond, Va., at a bakery that puts a spicy twist on a traditional Southern biscuit.

programme.title: Cheap Eats: Pit Stop
programme.desc: Pittsburgh has Ali Khan flipping over some cheesy pancakes, and battles a sub that might blow the classic Philly cheesesteak out of the water. He discovers the secrets to Pittsburgh's best pretzels.

programme.title: Good Eats: 'Twas the Night Before Good Eats
programme.desc: A dark holiday night finds Alton Brown visited by a series of culinary spirits that drive him to prepare long lost yuletide classics.

programme.title: Man Fire Food: Roadside 'Cue
programme.desc: While road-tripping through Texas, Roger Mooking pulls the car over for some seriously delicious barbecue. In San Antonio, he makes a pit stop at The Box Street Social food truck to hang chickens and racks of ribs over a live fire.

programme.title: Man Fire Food: Pit Bosses
programme.desc: Roger Mooking is honoring the legendary pitmasters who make some of the country's best barbecue.

programme.title: SEC Featured: LSU
programme.desc: A wrap-up of the latest SEC features where fans can see the stories beyond the sidelines.

programme.title: Homecoming With Willie Geist
programme.desc: Finebaum chats with Vanderbilt graduate, television personality and journalist Willie Geist.

programme.title: Homecoming With Titus O'Neil
programme.desc: Finebaum chats with former American football player and current professional wrestler Titus O'Neil.

programme.title: Homecoming With Tim Cook
programme.desc: Auburn alumnus and current Apple CEO Tim Cook discusses his time on campus and his memories of watching the Auburn Tigers.

programme.title: Homecoming With Marillyn Hewson
programme.desc: Paul Finebaum chats with Marillyn Hewson, the chairman, president, and chief executive officer of Lockheed Martin.

programme.title: Homecoming With Billy Payne
programme.desc: Paul Finebaum sits down with Billy Payne, an SEC alumnus, former Augusta National Golf Club chairman and CEO of the organizing committee for the 1996 Olympics.

programme.title: SEC Storied: Mighty Ruthie
programme.desc: Auburn Tiger and Olympic gold medalist Ruthie Bolton is a victim of domestic violence and uses her platform to become an advocate for women's empowerment.

programme.title: SEC Storied: Repeat After Us
programme.desc: Florida's big five return for the 2007 season to become the first school to repeat as basketball national champions with the same starting lineup.

programme.title: SEC Storied: Maravich
programme.desc: Explores the life of 'Pistol' Pete Maravich, one of the most prolific scorers and compelling personalities in basketball history.

programme.title: SEC Storied: The Rebel
programme.desc: The story of Johnny Neumann, a prodigy who let his ego get in the way of his success.

programme.title: SEC Storied: 40 Minutes of Hell
programme.desc: Look at the rise, fall and redemption of University of Arkansas coach Nolan Richardson.

programme.title: SEC Storied: Norm
programme.desc: Chronicling the life of Missouri men's basketball coaching legend Norm Stewart, centering on Stewart's life as an athlete, a husband, a coach and a cancer survivor.

programme.title: SEC Storied: Abby Head On
programme.desc: A look at soccer player Abby Wombach's perseverance to excel in her sport and become one of the best athletes the United States has ever produced.

programme.title: SEC Storied: The Stars Are Aligned
programme.desc: Fourteen celebrities and public figures each representing a different school from the Southeastern Conference talk about their emotional connection to their respective college and the SEC and explain why they'll never forget where they came from.

programme.title: SEC Storied: Sarah and Suzanne
programme.desc: Alabama's Sarah Patterson and Georgia's Suzanne Yoculan took their schools' gymnastics program from the verge of folding to the best in the country.

programme.title: SEC Storied: Lolo Jones
programme.desc: Lori 'Lolo' Jones was a standout hurdler from LSU where she won 3 NCAA titles and 11 All-American honors. Lolo explores her inspiring rise from an unstable home life to national stardom that included a spot on the 2008 United States Olympic team.

programme.title: SEC Storied: Dominique Belongs to Us
programme.desc: Dominique Wilkins was the toast of his hometown in Washington, N.C. After choosing the University of Georgia over local ACC schools, his fans' cheers turned to jeers. The betrayal left him distrustful of fans until fan support paved his way home.

programme.title: SEC Storied: Shaq & Dale
programme.desc: This film follows former basketball great Shaquille O'Neal as he returns to LSU to visit Dale Brown. The relationship forged between these two men goes back to when Shaq was 13 and since that day they have formed a deep friendship.

programme.title: SEC Storied: Miracle 3
programme.desc: The story of Mykal Riley's desperation three-point shot that sent the Crimson Tide into overtime of the 2008 SEC basketball tournament and saved thousands of fans from an oncoming tornado.

programme.title: SEC Storied: Scramblin' Fran
programme.desc: 'Scramblin'' Fran Tarkenton's path is chronicled from quarterback at Georgia to three Super Bowl championships with the Minnesota Vikings.

programme.title: SEC Storied: King George
programme.desc: George Washington Rogers, winner of the Heisman Trophy in 1980, tells the story of his life.

programme.title: SEC Storied: The Play That Changed College Football
programme.desc: Look at the inaugural SEC championship game in 1992 and the impact it's had on college football.

programme.desc: Jason Whitlock and Marcellus Wiley cover all of the headlines and most provocative stories from different angles.

programme.desc: The Plaintiff is suing her ex-boyfriend and claims he cheated on her with at least 20 women. Her claim is for an unpaid loan and the bail bond she had to pay after she had to bail him out of jail.

programme.title: The People's Court
programme.desc: The presiding judge serves as an arbitrator in these real-life small-claims court cases.

programme.title: The People's Court
programme.desc: The presiding judge serves as an arbitrator in these real-life small-claims court cases.

programme.title: Justice With Judge Mablean: Customer Showdown/ What's Normal?
programme.desc: Customer breaks fish display window; ex-employee sues boss over company holiday.

programme.title: Judge Jerry
programme.desc: A man sues his ex-wife for a propane gas bill and rent; the defendant says paying the bills is the least he can do since she takes car of his kids from a previous marriage. Then, plaintiffs sue for unpaid wages and fuel costs.

programme.title: The Steve Wilkos Show: DNA: She Was Only 13
programme.desc: Allison is just 15 years old with a 10-month-old son. Her ex-boyfriend, Matthew, has denied being the father of the child since the inception.

programme.title: Maury: Hairy Holiday Maury Makeovers!
programme.desc: People who've been scorned and ridiculed during the lives get a Maury makeover. Don't miss the laughs, tears an reveals.

programme.title: The Jerry Springer Show: I Sexed Your Ex
programme.desc: Randal says there's nothing he won't do to be with the woman he loves named Kayla. She apparently dumped him for no reason and he demands to know why.

programme.title: Movie: Without a Paddle
programme.desc: Inspired by their friend's sudden death, three life-long friends go on a journey to fulfill their long-time dream of finding a lost fortune in the Oregon Mountains.

programme.title: Movie: Cyrus
programme.desc: After dealing with his divorce, a man finally meets the woman of his dreams, only to discover that she is oddly close to her 21-year-old musician son.

programme.title: Movie: Rebound
programme.desc: A college basketball coach, who was banned from the court until he could curb his temper, accepts a position with a helpless team and tries to turn them around.

programme.title: Movie: The Darjeeling Limited
programme.desc: Three brothers reunite and set out on a spiritual journey to find themselves.

programme.title: Movie: The Hudsucker Proxy
programme.desc: A naïve Indiana boy goes to New York in 1958 to sell his idea for the hula hoop.

programme.title: Movie: Sex and the City 2
programme.desc: When Samantha is offered an all-expenses paid trip to Abu Dhabi, Carrie, Miranda and Charlotte trade in their day-to-day lives for a little rest, relaxation and shopping in the beautiful United Arab Emirates.

programme.title: Movie: Wild Hogs
programme.desc: A group of middle-aged men try to put their troubles behind them as they set out on a road trip on their Harleys.

programme.title: Movie: True Lies
programme.desc: A secret agent on the trail of a group of terrorists puts his family at risk when they discover his true identity.

programme.title: Movie: The Ladykillers
programme.desc: A group of thieves are plotting to rob a casino and hide out in an elderly woman's basement.

programme.title: Movie: Blindspotting
programme.desc: With only three days left on his probation, Collin is determined to avoid even a small hint of illegal activity, but with his unstable best friend Miles causing trouble the two end up on a collision course that could jeopardize Collin's freedom.

programme.title: Movie: Notting Hill
programme.desc: The press tests the relationship between a Hollywood star and a London shopkeeper.

programme.title: Movie: My Big Fat Greek Wedding
programme.desc: A Greek girl falls in love with a non-Greek man, which creates havoc amongst her family.

programme.title: Movie: Nine Months
programme.desc: A child psychiatrist panics when his girlfriend announces that she is pregnant.

programme.desc: When the Clampett family strikes oil in the Ozark swamps, they move to Beverly Hills.
